author: pk910
content:
I got a couple of changes for the system contract to think about:

First: I think it makes sense to not hard-code the dequeue rate at 256 deposits/slot. That's more like a finger in the air value we've agreed on, but don't know if it's too low or too high in future.
I think it's a simple addition to pass the dequeue rate via the system call on each block,  so we can change the rate in a later fork without re-deploying the contract or doing a irregular state change:
https://github.com/wemeetagain/EIPs/commit/e04626092adffa461e2d6c9303f217e22918169e

Second, as we talked about, making the fee adjusting per tx instead of once per block might make sense.  Fixes the "fillup queue and let the fee spike to the hudrets range in one block"-issue:
https://github.com/wemeetagain/EIPs/commit/c484038b07a973064b41f5efb6c1b11bbebc7623

And third (+4th), just for completeness, the addition of the enqueue timestamp, so we can onboard from the queue right from fork boundary :
https://github.com/wemeetagain/EIPs/commit/16b55e525fa93558004ae3f51fc799e59eeecbba
and related (depends on 2nd), allow pre-fork deposits: https://github.com/wemeetagain/EIPs/commit/1f3eaf492009d93544fb9f74be0bee11318d8bc8


I'm fine with adding none of these for keeping the diff minimal.  But none of these changes seem utterly complex on their own, so we should at least have them as an option
